title: Uragoga corniculata
intro: Uragoga corniculata, also known as Urotago, is a plant species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and various gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Uragoga corniculata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uragoga corniculata include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
